prompt_text: Semgrep vs SonarQube pricing and features
response:
Semgrep and SonarQube differ both in **pricing model** and **feature focus**: Semgrep uses mainly **per-developer pricing** with a generous free tier and focuses on **security (SAST + SCA + secrets) and speed**, while SonarQube prices mainly **by lines of code (LOC)** and is stronger on **code quality, breadth of languages, and governance**.[1][2][3]

Below is a concise side‑by‑side comparison focused on pricing and key capabilities.

---

### 1. Pricing model & typical costs

#### Semgrep

- **Model**
  - **Free / OSS:**
    - Semgrep **Community Edition**: free, open source, no usage limits for self‑hosted.[3]
    - **Hosted free tier**: up to **10 contributors and 50 repos** with Pro engine, Pro rules, SCA, and AI triage.[2][3]
  - **Commercial platform (hosted or self‑managed):**
    - **Team plan:** about **$35/month per contributor** for **Code** and **$35/month per contributor** for **Supply Chain (SCA)**, plus **$15/month per contributor** for **Secrets**.[2][1]
    - **Enterprise:** custom pricing; includes unlimited repos, on‑prem SCM, and enterprise support.[2]

- **Real‑world contract sizes**
  - Median annual contract about **$58.8K/year** (range **$20K–$193K/year**) across 41 purchases, with ~29% average discount.[2]
  - A 200‑developer team pays roughly **$96K–$120K/year** for **Semgrep Code** alone at list price.[1]

- **Pros / Cons of Semgrep pricing**
  - **Pros:** Predictable per‑developer scaling; very capable **free tier** (10 contributors) including advanced features.[2]
  - **Cons:** For large engineering orgs with hundreds of developers, per‑developer pricing can surpass SonarQube Enterprise for a similar LOC footprint.[1][2]

#### SonarQube

- **Model**
  - **Community Build:** free, open source (LGPL‑3.0), per‑instance, limited to main branch, 20–21+ languages, no taint analysis or PR decoration.[1][2][3]
  - **Paid self‑hosted editions (per instance, priced by LOC):**[1][2][3][5]
    - **Developer Edition:** starts around **$720/year at 100K LOC**.[2]
    - Often quoted ~**$2,500/year at 500K–100K LOC** in practitioner reports.[2]
    - **Enterprise Edition:** roughly **$16K–$20K/year at 1M LOC**, scaling up with LOC.[1][2]
    - **Data Center Edition:** around **$100K–$130K+/year** for 10M+ LOC and HA setups.[1][2]
  - **SonarQube Cloud / SonarCloud:**
    - Free up to **50K LOC**; paid “Team” plan from about **€30/month**.[1]

- **Discount reality**
  - Enterprise at **5M LOC** list around **$35.7K/year**, with achievable discounts bringing effective cost to **$19K–$25K/year**.[2]
  - Multi‑year deals can secure **70%+ discounts**.[2]

- **Pros / Cons of SonarQube pricing**
  - **Pros:** For large codebases with many developers, LOC‑based pricing often ends up **cheaper than per‑developer** models.[1][2]
  - **Cons:** LOC growth can make costs hard to predict; SCA and some advanced security capabilities require higher (Enterprise) tiers.[2][5]

---

### 2. High‑level feature positioning

| Aspect | **Semgrep** | **SonarQube** |
|---|---|---|
|Primary focus | **Security‑focused SAST + SCA + secrets**[3] | **Code quality + security** with strong governance[3][8] |
|Pricing basis | **Per contributor** (commercial) + free contributor‑limited tier[2] | **Per LOC** (self‑hosted editions) + free Community[1][2][3] |
|Free tier | CE + hosted free for **10 contributors / 50 repos** w/ Pro features[2][3] | **Community Build**: free, 20+ languages, main branch only[1][3] |
|Best economic fit | Teams with moderate developer count and/or lots of security use; early‑stage and mid‑size orgs | Large codebases, many devs, and orgs needing strong **code
